method.category_support: Checked every user issue against an independent category-specific semantic-anchor vocabulary covering access, billing, malfunction, cancellation, requested behavior, shipping, and security intent.
method.priority_support: Required the user issue to contain exactly the impact cue associated with its expected priority and none of the other three impact cues.
method.sentiment_support: Required the user issue to contain exactly the wording cue associated with its expected sentiment and none of the other four sentiment cues.
method.next_action_policy: Validated the action syntax and required category-specific routing prefixes: access verify/review/inspect; billing review/trace/apply/restore; bug triage; cancellation and feature review; shipping trace/review; security secure/review/revoke/lock.
method.response_safety: Required future-tense review and safe-routing language, rejected completed-resolution claims, and rejected identifiers or placeholders in responses.
method.pii_safety: Compared declared placeholders with placeholder use and scanned placeholder-redacted user text plus responses for email, IPv4, SSN, payment-card, phone-number, and credential-value patterns.
method.consistency: Required the assistant JSON to equal the expected object and verified the fixed synthetic, CC0-1.0, no-real-customer-data provenance declaration.
limitations: This is a deterministic automated policy audit, not a human judgment review.
Pattern-based PII checks reduce risk but cannot prove that no sensitive information exists.
Passing this audit does not approve, qualify, train, merge, deploy, or release the dataset or a model.

qualification_notice: Automated audit B found no policy violations in its defined checks. It is not human review and has no release-qualification effect.
